# Republic Studio - GitHub Setup Guide\n\nThis guide turns GitHub into **Republic Studio**, the central hub for all development.\n\n## Overview\n\nRepublic Studio uses GitHub as the complete project management system:\n- **Issues** = Task Management\n- **Project Boards** = Sprint Planning\n- **Discussions** = Design & Strategy\n- **CI/CD** = Automated Verification\n- **Docs** = Source of Truth\n\n---\n\n## Part 1: Create Project Boards\n\n### Setup Instructions\n\n1. **Go to Repository**\n   - Navigate to: https://github.com/Don-Walesky/Republic-Game\n   - Click the **Projects** tab\n   - Click **New Project**\n\n2. **Create Each Phase Board**\n\nCreate 6 separate project boards, one per phase:\n\n#### Board 1: Phase 1 - Foundation\n- **Title**: Phase 1 - Foundation\n- **Description**: Core infrastructure and engine systems (2-3 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Add Custom Field**: Priority (High, Medium, Low)\n- **Add Custom Field**: Story Points (1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13)\n- **Add Custom Field**: Assigned Dev\n\n#### Board 2: Phase 2 - World Building\n- **Title**: Phase 2 - World Building\n- **Description**: Geographic, demographic, and economic foundations (3-4 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n#### Board 3: Phase 3 - Advanced Systems\n- **Title**: Phase 3 - Advanced Systems\n- **Description**: Economy, Politics, and Military (6-8 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n#### Board 4: Phase 4 - Intelligence\n- **Title**: Phase 4 - Intelligence\n- **Description**: AI and autonomous decision-making (4-6 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n#### Board 5: Phase 5 - Multiplayer\n- **Title**: Phase 5 - Multiplayer\n- **Description**: Networking and multiplayer support (4-6 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n#### Board 6: Phase 6 - Presentation\n- **Title**: Phase 6 - Presentation\n- **Description**: UI and visualization (4-6 weeks)\n- **Template**: Table view\n- **Same Custom Fields**\n\n### Configure Each Board with Columns\n\nFor each board, set up columns:\n\n```\n┌──────────────┬─────────────┬────────────┬──────────────┬────────┐\n│   Backlog    │  Not Started│ In Progress│ In Review    │  Done  │\n├──────────────┼─────────────┼────────────┼──────────────┼────────┤\n│ Not assigned │ Ready to    │ Actively   │ PR/testing   │ Ready  │\n│ to sprint    │ start, no   │ being      │ phase        │ for    │\n│              │ blockers    │ worked on  │              │ next   │\n│              │             │            │              │ phase  │\n└──────────────┴─────────────┴────────────┴──────────────┴────────┘\n```\n\n### Add Issues to Boards\n\n1. Go to each Issue (EPIC-01.1, EPIC-01.2, etc)\n2. Click \"Projects\" in the right sidebar\n3. Select the appropriate board (e.g., \"Phase 1 - Foundation\" for EPIC-01)\n4. Add to \"Backlog\" column\n5. Set Priority, Story Points, and assign Dev\n\n---\n\n## Part 2: Master Sprint Board\n\nCreate one additional **Master Sprint Board** for cross-phase visibility:\n\n### Setup\n\n1. **Create Project**: \"Active Sprint - Republic Studio\"\n2. **View Type**: Table\n3. **Columns**: \n   - This Week\n   - In Progress\n   - Blocked\n   - Under Review\n   - Complete\n\n4. **Filters**: Show all open issues assigned to sprint\n\nThis gives a quick \"pulse\" view of what's happening right now.\n\n---\n\n## Part 3: Connect Issues to Boards\n\n### Existing Issues (Already Created)\n\nOur 10 Epic issues need to be added to project boards:\n\n```\nPhase 1 - Foundation Board:\n├─ EPIC-01.1: Project Bootstrap & Build Pipeline (#?)\n├─ EPIC-01.2: Logging System (#?)\n├─ EPIC-01.3: Event Bus System (#10)\n├─ EPIC-01.4: Time System (#9)\n├─ EPIC-01.5: Save System (#6)\n├─ EPIC-01.6: World Manager (#8)\n├─ EPIC-01.7: Country Entity (#7)\n├─ EPIC-01.8: Configuration System (#?)\n└─ EPIC-02.6: Constitution System (#5)\n\nPhase 2 - World Building Board:\n├─ EPIC-02.1: Country Generation\n├─ EPIC-02.2: Geography System\n├─ EPIC-02.3: Population System\n├─ EPIC-02.4: Resources System\n├─ EPIC-02.5: Currency System\n└─ EPIC-02.7: Political Culture System\n```\n\n### Future Issues\n\nAs you create new features/bugs:\n1. Tag with appropriate Phase label\n2. Add to corresponding project board\n3. Set Priority and Story Points\n4. Assign to developer\n\n---\n\n## Part 4: Workflow\n\n### Daily\n\n1. **Check Active Sprint Board**\n   - See what's in progress\n   - Identify blockers\n   - Update status\n\n2. **Move Issues Across Columns**\n   - Started? → Move to \"In Progress\"\n   - Ready for review? → Move to \"In Review\"\n   - Merged? → Move to \"Done\"\n\n### Weekly Sprint Planning\n\n1. **Open Phase Board** (e.g., \"Phase 1 - Foundation\")\n2. **Review Backlog**\n   - What's ready to start?\n   - What's blocked?\n   - What needs clarification?\n3. **Move to \"Not Started\"** the issues for this sprint\n4. **Assign developers**\n5. **Set story points** for effort estimation\n\n### Phase Completion\n\n1. **Move all issues to \"Done\"**\n2. **Close milestone** for that phase\n3. **Create release notes**\n4. **Archive board**\n5. **Move to next phase board**\n\n---\n\n## Part 5: Board Automation\n\n### Enable Auto-Draft Issues\n\n1. Go to each project board\n2. **Settings** → **Automation**\n3. Enable:\n   - Auto-add new issues to \"Backlog\"\n   - Auto-move PRs to \"In Review\" when PR created\n   - Auto-move to \"Done\" when PR merged\n\n### GitHub Actions Integration\n\nCreate workflow to auto-update issues:\n- Automatically comment on issues when PR created\n- Close related issues when PR merged\n- Tag issues with phase labels\n\n---\n\n## Part 6: Custom Fields Guide\n\n### Priority\n- **Critical (P0)**: Blocks other work, essential for phase\n- **High (P1)**: Important, should do this phase\n- **Medium (P2)**: Nice to have, can defer\n- **Low (P3)**: Polish, can skip if time runs out\n\n### Story Points\n- **1**: Trivial (< 1 hour)\n- **2**: Small (1-2 hours)\n- **3**: Medium (half day)\n- **5**: Large (1-2 days)\n- **8**: Very Large (3-5 days)\n- **13**: Epic-sized (> 1 week)\n\n### Assigned Dev\n- Select team member responsible\n- Can be left unassigned for backlog\n- Must be assigned before moving to \"In Progress\"\n\n---\n\n## Part 7: Board Views\n\n### Table View (Recommended for Sprint Planning)\n- Shows all columns\n- Sortable by Priority, Story Points, Status\n- Can bulk-edit fields\n- Best for: Weekly planning, velocity tracking\n\n### Board View (Kanban style)\n- Visual columns\n- Drag-and-drop between columns\n- Shows issue cards\n- Best for: Daily standup, flow visualization\n\n### Roadmap View\n- Shows timeline\n- Milestones on calendar\n- Displays phase schedule\n- Best for: Long-term planning, stakeholder view\n\n---\n\n## Part 8: Reporting & Metrics\n\n### Velocity Tracking\n1. Complete issues each sprint\n2. Sum up story points\n3. Track trend (should stabilize)\n4. Use for future sprint planning\n\n### Burndown Chart\n1. Count remaining story points daily\n2. Plot on graph\n3. Should trend downward by sprint end\n4. Identifies if sprint is on-track\n\n### Phase Progress\n1. Total issues per phase\n2. % Complete (Done / Total)\n3. Estimated completion date\n4. Status: On Track / At Risk / Blocked\n\n---\n\n## Part 9: Linking to Documentation\n\n### In Each Issue, Include:\n\n```markdown\n## Design Reference\n- Design Principles: [Link to relevant principle]\n- Feature Spec: [Link to feature documentation]\n- UI Design: [Link to UX document]\n- Architecture: [Link to architecture doc]\n\n## Republic Test\n- [ ] Strengthens executive leadership experience?\n- [ ] Creates meaningful strategic choices?\n- [ ] Affects multiple systems?\n- [ ] Fits office-centric design?\n- [ ] Increases replayability?\n- [ ] Enables emergent storytelling?\n- [ ] Reinforces player role?\n```\n\nThis keeps documentation connected to implementation.\n\n---\n\n## Part 10: Success Criteria\n\n**Republic Studio is working when:**\n\n✅ All phase work visible on appropriate board\n✅ Developers move issues as they work\n✅ Sprint board updated daily\n✅ Issues linked to design docs\n✅ Velocity tracked and trending\n✅ Blockers identified and visible\n✅ Team communicates status via GitHub (no external chat)\n✅ Phase completion automated via CI/CD\n✅ Discussions used for design decisions (not chat)\n✅ Everything lives in GitHub (one source of truth)\n\n---\n\n## Next Steps\n\n1. ✅ Create 6 phase boards\n2. ✅ Create master sprint board\n3. ✅ Add existing issues to boards\n4. ✅ Set up custom fields\n5. ✅ Configure automation\n6. ✅ Start tracking velocity\n7. ✅ Link issues to documentation\n8. ✅ Run first sprint\n\n**Then move to:**\n- Part 2: Issue Templates\n- Part 3: Labels System\n- Part 4: GitHub Actions CI/CD\n- Part 5: Discussion Categories\n\n---\n\n*Republic Studio: Everything in GitHub. One source of truth. Pure studio discipline.*\n"
